expressions.notes:
왼손 = left (side)
왼 is the adjective form of 왼쪽.

오른쪽 = right (side)
오른 is the adjective form of 오른쪽.
들다 = to raise; to pick (something) up
대다 = to touch; to apply something to something

expressions.english: I will go wash my hands.
expressions.notes:
씻다 = to wash
오다 = to come

참 = very, quite
예쁘다 = to be pretty

expressions.english: Don't you have hands? Do it yourself!
expressions.notes:
너 = (casual) you
니 is originally 너, but people often just use 니 in casual conversations.

expressions.english: What is that thing that you have in your hand?
expressions.notes:
뭐 = what; something
뭐 is the contracted form of 무엇.

뜨겁다 = to be hot
조심하다 = to be careful

expressions.english: I made it with my own hands.
expressions.notes:
직접 = directly; by oneself; with one's own hands
만들다 = to make

expressions.english: Don't rub your eyes with your hands.
expressions.notes:
눈 = eye
비비다 = to rub
-지 마세요 = (polite) Don't + V

expressions.english: Yesterday, we held hands for the first time.

Don't touch it with dirty hands.
Don't touch that with your dirty hands.
expressions.notes:
더럽다 = to be dirty
더러운 is the adjective form of 더럽다.
만지다 = to touch

expressions.korean: 정답을 아시는 분은 손 들어 주세요.
expressions.english: If you know the correct answer, please raise your hand.
expressions.notes:
정답 = correct answer
아시다 = (honorific) to know
분 = (honorific) person
